flags.detail: 438 FAIL_TO_PASS tests, where a normal task flips only one or two. A focused bug fix does not turn hundreds of tests green; a pass here reflects a sweeping change, and a partial score is dominated by test volume rather than the fix.

2 of 412 test ids are corrupt - SWE-bench's log parser truncated them at a space, e.g. 'lib/matplotlib/tests/test_axes.py::test_stem[png-w/'. pytest aborts the whole run on an unknown id, so the task would falsely score 0; the harness quarantines them before scoring.

3 of 64 test ids are corrupt - SWE-bench's log parser truncated them at a space, e.g. 'testing/test_unittest.py::test_setup_inheritance_skipping[test_setup_skip.py-1'. pytest aborts the whole run on an unknown id, so the task would falsely score 0; the harness quarantines them before scoring.

1 of 109 test ids are corrupt - pytest progress-bar output was captured as a test id: '[100%]'. pytest aborts the whole run on an unknown id, so the task would falsely score 0; the harness quarantines them before scoring.

the problem statement quotes the gold fix verbatim: "elif not hasattr(estimator, 'get_params') or isinstance(estimator, type):". A pass here shows the agent can copy a line from the prompt, not that it can solve the bug.
